name: OpenSAFELY
description: A secure analytics platform for electronic health records, enabling approved researchers to run analyses against GP data from 55M+ patients in England. Pioneered a transparency-first, code-runs-on-data model where researchers submit analysis code via GitHub and outputs are reviewed before release.
tre_platform: OpenSAFELY platform (code submitted via GitHub, runs on backends)

requirements.name: GitHub repository
requirements.description: All analysis code must be in a public GitHub repository for full transparency

requirements.name: Study definition in code
requirements.description: Research questions defined as executable code using the OpenSAFELY cohort-extractor

requirements.name: Output review
requirements.description: All outputs undergo disclosure review before release from the secure environment

requirements.name: Pre-print commitment
requirements.description: Commitment to publish results as pre-prints for transparency

requirements.name: Coding skills
requirements.description: Researchers must be able to write Python/R analysis code

access_steps.name: Submit study proposal
access_steps.description: Describe research question, proposed analysis, and public benefit

access_steps.name: Develop code on synthetic data
access_steps.description: Write and test analysis code against synthetic data in the open

access_steps.name: Code review
access_steps.description: OpenSAFELY team reviews code for governance and technical compliance

access_steps.name: Run on real data
access_steps.description: Approved code executed against real patient data in the secure backend

access_steps.name: Output review and release
access_steps.description: Results undergo disclosure checking before release

timeline.notes: Code development on synthetic data - duration depends on complexity

costs.notes: Free for approved public-good research. Expanding funding sources beyond COVID.

strengths: Fastest turnaround of any major platform
limitations: No data extraction - code must run within platform
Not available for purely commercial research
recent_changes: EMIS backend now available alongside TPP
Broadening beyond COVID to wider disease areas with Wellcome funding
